title: Gate step output
description: Result of a gate_step decision by a human (or other principal). Persisted as the durable record of the approval / rejection. Engine maps decision → transition via the gate_step's outputs map.

approver.description: Username, role label, or agent identifier of who decided.

comment.description: Optional free-form note explaining the decision. Audited but does not affect transition.

form_data.description: Values submitted via the gate's form fields, keyed by field.name. Strings (text/textarea/select) or booleans (checkbox).

deferral_approvals.description: When the close_gate decision is `approved` and the upstream final_verification table contains `unverified` AC rows, each unverified row MUST have a corresponding deferral approval entry here. Engine rejects approval if rows are missing. Skipped when no unverified rows exist.

concern_triage.description: When the gate-summary surfaced concerns at this gate, each concern MUST be triaged here before the engine accepts an `approved` decision. The triage record drives ticket write-backs and routing: `fix_in_this_ticket` forces the gate to reject so the implementer can address the concern in the current ticket; `accept` echoes the text into `# Out of scope`; `defer` records a follow-up_ticket pointer in `# Resolution`; `dismiss` stays in the gate-decision JSON only.

concern.description: The concern text as surfaced by gate-summary (verbatim or PdM-paraphrased).

action.description: `fix_in_this_ticket` = real concern, address it in the current ticket (engine rejects approve and routes back to implement with the rationale as a structured fix item); `accept` = real concern, consciously left as-is (Out of scope); `defer` = real concern, will fix in a separate follow-up ticket; `dismiss` = not a real concern / LLM false positive.

rationale.description: One-line reason. Persists to the gate decision JSON and (for fix_in_this_ticket / accept / defer) to the ticket or implementer's fix list.

follow_up_ticket.description: Required when action=defer. Slug of an existing or to-be-cut follow-up ticket.
